article_title: GitLab 18.11 Ships Agentic CI Setup, Autonomous Vulnerability Fixes, and Live Analytics as Duo Agent Platform Matures

content_preview.title: GitLab 18.11 Ships Agentic CI Setup, Autonomous Vulnerability Fixes, and Live Analytics as Duo Agent Platform Matures
content_preview.summary: GitLab's April release adds a CI Expert Agent that generates pipelines from repository analysis, moves Agentic SAST vulnerability resolution to GA, and delivers natural-language DevOps analytics.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

GitLab released version 18.11 on April 16, 2026, marking the most significant expansion of its Duo Agent Platform since the agentic tooling entered general availability in early 2026. The release introduces two new foundational agents — one that generates CI/CD pipelines from a repository scan, and one that delivers natural-language answers over live development metrics — alongside the general availability of automated vulnerability remediation that writes and opens its own merge re...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Thorough, well-structured release coverage. Good use of official docs and press release alongside third-party coverage. Appropriate technical depth for a developer tools audience.
editor_notes.source_verification: Fetched all 4 sources. GitLab press release confirms CI Expert Agent (beta), Agentic SAST GA for Ultimate, Data Analyst Agent GA across all tiers, 11 hours/month remediation stat from 2025 DevSecOps Report. Help Net Security confirms the same stat. Official release notes confirm ClickHouse backend, Kubernetes 1.35, PostgreSQL 17, Claude Sonnet 4.6 upgrade from Haiku 4.5, CVSS 4.0, EPSS, Maven/Python dependency scanning. Agentic SAST docs confirm GitLab 18.9 minimum, group/project configuration requirements, multi-shot reasoning, false positive handling.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All major technical claims verified across sources. Minor note: the 'context-switching between security tools and code editors' elaboration in the SAST paragraph is a reasonable editorial inference from the 11-hour stat but is not explicitly stated in the cited Help Net Security source — acceptable given the clarity of attribution. Confidence score documentation confirms the feature exists but notes calculation methodology is not publicly disclosed, which the article correctly flags in the 'What We Don't Know' section.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Good competitive framing in the Analysis section without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: No prior GitLab 18.11 coverage in the archive.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, well-sourced release article. All technical claims verified. Approved for publication.
